title: Amid ‘Cancel ChatGPT’ trend, Anthropic launches feature to help users switch to Claude
summary: Anthropic has launched a new import memory feature for Claude which allows paid users to bring memories from other AI chatbots. The trend comes at a time when Anthropic has seen strong public sentiment in its favour following cancellation of its deal with the US government.

title: Apple's ‘big week’ announcements begin from today today: iPhone 17e, new iPads, budget MacBook and everything expected
summary: Apple plans to unveil several new products this week, including the iPhone 17e, new MacBooks, and iPads. CEO Tim Cook has hinted at a multi-day launch starting March 2 which could conclude with the event on 4 March.

title: Nothing Phone 4a and 4a Pro launching on 5 March: Design, expected specs and more
summary: Nothing is set to launch its Phone 4 (a) series on 5 March. The launch event is also likely to see the unveling of new Headphone (a) with bold colors and long battery life. The Phone 4a will likely include a Snapdragon 7s Gen 4 processor, a triple camera setup along with a 6.78 inch OLED display.

title: OpenAI defends rival Anthropic against Pentagon ban, Sam Altman calls it ‘extremely scary precedent’
summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman expressed concerns over the US government's ban on rival Anthropic, labeling it a scary precedent. Despite OpenAI's past tensions with Anthropic, Altman defended the rival AI lab during a recent AMA session.

title: Did Grok predict US attack on Iran? Elon Musk claims victory for xAI after viral test
summary: &nbsp;Elon Musk's Grok chatbot uniquely predicted the date of the US strike on Iran as 28 February 2026, even prior to the attack. The prediction was made by the AI chatbot in a methodological test on 25 February.

title: Anthropic's Claude AI hits number 1 spot on App Store as users boycott OpenAI's new Pentagon deal
summary: Anthropic's Claude has jumped to the number 1 spot on the Apple App Store hours after being banned by the US government. Meanwhile, OpenAI's ChatGPT is once again facing calls for boycott and subscription cancellations after its deal with the US government.&nbsp;

title: Trump banned Anthropic — hours later, US military used its Claude AI in Iran strikes: Report
summary: Despite announcing a phase-out, the US used Anthropic's AI during an airstrike on Iran. The technology supported intelligence and target identification, underscoring its significance in military operations, including previous use in capturing Nicolás Maduro.

title: Amid Israel-Iran conflict, FlightRadar24 starts showing error due to ‘unprecedented’ volume
summary: Flightradar24 reported unprecedented traffic on its platform following US strikes on Iran. The company acknowledged site availability issues and stated that engineers are actively working to resolve them, thanking users for their patience.

title: Xiaomi 17 Ultra with Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 SoC, 6,330 mAh battery launched globally: Price, specs and more
summary: Xiaomi has unveiled the Xiaomi 17 Ultra in Barcelona, featuring a Leica triple camera, Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5, and a 6.9-inch OLED display. Prices start at 1,499 Euros for the base model, with IP68 rating and a 6,000mAh battery.

title: Xiaomi 17 with triple 50MP Leica cameras, 6,330mAh battery launched in India: Price, specs and more
summary: Xiaomi launched the flagship Xiaomi 17 in Barcelona, competing with Oppo Find X9 and OnePlus 15. It offers four colors, starts at 999 Euros, features a 6.3-inch OLED display, Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 processor, Leica triple camera setup, and a 6,330mAh battery with fast charging.

title: Anthropic’s Claude chatbot climbs to no.1 on Apple’s US App Store amid Pentagon contract row
summary: Anthropic’s chatbot Claude has reached number one in the free rankings on the US Apple App Store, overtaking OpenAI’s ChatGPT, with record registrations and rising subscriptions. The milestone comes amid tensions with the Pentagon after Donald Trump halted federal use of Anthropic’s systems over nat

title: Iran crisis hits India’s economy; PE firms dial up SaaS deals
summary: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East is likely to significantly impact Indian industries. This and more in today’s ETtech Morning Dispatch.

title: Iran-Israel war: Companies could see rise in digital threats, say experts
summary: Iran-Israel war: Cybersecurity experts and consultants have issued advisories to Indian corporates warning that hackers could attempt to attack critical infrastructure including power grids, telecommunication networks, banking ATMs, as observed during Operation Sindoor.

title: Iran-Israel war: Electronics exports of $4.5 billion at risk
summary: Iran-Israel war: The UAE was the second-largest buyer of Indian electronic products, according to data from the commerce and industry ministry. In the first nine months (April-December) of FY26, the UAE was the destination for $4.1 billion of electronics exports.

title: PE firms up SaaS buyouts as AI resets valuations & biz models
summary: Over the past year, multiple mid-to-late stage SaaS companies have seen majority stake purchases by private equity or have consolidated internally in what is a shift from the typical venture-led growth rounds from a few years ago.
